title: Insight loading spinner never disappears when query times out
description:
When running a complex funnel query with 8+ steps and a 90-day date range, the loading spinner keeps spinning indefinitely. The query seems to time out on the backend (I see a 504 in the network tab after \~60s) but the frontend never shows an error state.

Steps to reproduce:

1. Create a new funnel insight
2. Add 8 or more steps with various property filters
3. Set date range to "Last 90 days"
4. Click "Calculate"

Expected: Error message after timeout
Actual: Infinite spinner, have to refresh the page

This is really frustrating because I lose my funnel configuration when I refresh.

title: Feature request: allow exporting cohort as CSV with person properties
description:
We use PostHog cohorts extensively for marketing campaigns. Currently, when I export a cohort, I only get the distinct IDs. I need to be able to include person properties like email, name, and company in the CSV export.

Our workflow today:

1. Create a cohort in PostHog based on behavioral filters
2. Export the cohort
3. Manually cross-reference with our CRM to get contact info
4. Upload to our email tool

If we could select which person properties to include in the export, it would save us about 2 hours per campaign. We run \~4 campaigns per week so this adds up quickly.

Would also be useful to have a scheduled export option so we can set up recurring campaign lists.

title: Session replay search returns no results for known session IDs
description:
I'm trying to find a specific session replay by its session ID. I copy the session_id from our server logs and paste it into the session replay search bar, but it returns zero results even though I can see the session exists when I navigate to it directly via URL.

Session ID: 018e4f2a-3b1c-7000-8000-1a2b3c4d5e6f
Direct URL works: /replay/018e4f2a-3b1c-7000-8000-1a2b3c4d5e6f
Search returns: "No recordings found"

This happens consistently. I've tried with multiple session IDs. The search seems to only work with person names/emails but not session IDs, which makes debugging specific user-reported issues really painful.

Using PostHog Cloud, latest version.

title: Dashboard filters don't persist after page refresh
description:
When I apply date range or property filters on a dashboard and then refresh the page, all filters reset to their defaults. This is particularly annoying during team standups when I'm presenting metrics — if I accidentally refresh or navigate away, I lose my filter context.

Environment: PostHog Cloud
Browser: Chrome 122

I expected dashboard filter state to be preserved in the URL (like insights do) so I can bookmark filtered views and share them with teammates.

title: HogQL: substring function silently returns wrong results for multibyte characters
description:
We're using HogQL to extract parts of user-agent strings and noticed incorrect results when the string contains multibyte UTF-8 characters (e.g., emoji in custom properties).

```sql
SELECT substring(properties.$browser_language, 1, 5) FROM events WHERE timestamp > now() - interval 1 day
```

When `$browser_language` contains something like "日本語-JP", the substring returns garbled bytes instead of the expected "日本語-J". It seems like the function is operating on bytes rather than characters.

This is a data correctness issue — we're building reports based on language segmentation and getting wrong groupings because of this. No error is raised, the results just silently come back wrong.

title: Q1 2025 release checklist
description:
Tracking issue for Q1 release items.

- [ ] Finalize changelog
- [ ] Update version numbers
- [ ] Run regression test suite
- [ ] Deploy to staging
- [ ] QA sign-off
- [ ] Deploy to production
- [ ] Publish blog post
- [ ] Send changelog email

title: Bump posthog-js from 1.130.0 to 1.141.2
description:
Routine dependency update. posthog-js 1.141.2 includes several minor fixes.

Changelog: [https://github.com/PostHog/posthog-js/releases](<https://github.com/PostHog/posthog-js/releases>)

No breaking changes expected.

title: Feature flags: percentage rollout doesn't respect group properties
description:
We have a feature flag set up with a 50% rollout targeting companies (group type: "company"). The flag is supposed to be enabled for 50% of companies, but we're seeing inconsistent behavior where some users within an enabled company see the flag as off.

After investigating, it appears the percentage rollout is being calculated per-person rather than per-group when using group-based feature flags. This means within a single company, different users get different flag values, which defeats the purpose of group-based flags.

Flag configuration:

* Key: `new-billing-page`
* Rollout: 50%
* Match: Group type "company" where plan = "enterprise"

We verified by checking flag evaluations for 10 users in the same company — 6 got true and 4 got false. All should be the same.

This is blocking our enterprise feature rollout. We need group-level consistency for feature flags.

title: Improve correlation analysis to surface statistical significance
description:
The correlation analysis feature in funnels is useful but it's hard to know which correlations are actually meaningful vs. noise. Currently it shows a "correlation score" but doesn't indicate statistical significance.

Suggestion: Add a p-value or confidence interval to each correlation result. Color-code or badge results that meet a significance threshold (e.g., p < 0.05). This would help us focus on correlations that are actually worth investigating rather than spending time on spurious patterns.

We have a data science team that keeps asking "is this significant?" every time I share correlation results from PostHog, and I can never give them a confident answer.

title: How to track revenue events with posthog-node?
description:
I'm trying to set up revenue tracking using the Node.js SDK but the docs are unclear about the best approach. Specifically:

1. Should I send revenue as an event property on a custom event (e.g., `purchase_completed` with `$revenue` property)?
2. Or is there a dedicated revenue tracking API?
3. How do I handle different currencies? We process payments in USD, EUR, and GBP.
4. Can I attach the Stripe payment intent ID as a property for reconciliation?

The docs mention using `$set` for person properties but I'm not sure if revenue should be a person property (lifetime value) or an event property (per-transaction) or both.

Currently using posthog-node v3.6.2. Would appreciate a clear example for a typical e-commerce purchase event.

title: Batch exports silently drop events when ClickHouse connection pool is exhausted
description:
We noticed gaps in our batch export data to S3. After investigation, when the ClickHouse connection pool hits its limit during a large export, the export worker silently continues without the failed batch rather than retrying or raising an error.

We had a 3-hour window where roughly 40% of events were missing from our S3 export. The batch export job showed as "completed" in the UI with no errors, which made this very hard to diagnose.

Root cause: The connection pool timeout exception was being caught by a broad except clause that logged a warning but continued iteration. Fixed by narrowing the exception handler and adding a retry with backoff for connection pool exhaustion specifically.

This was fixed in PR #18234 by adding explicit retry logic for pool exhaustion errors and surfacing a clear error state when retries are exhausted.

title: Add ability to duplicate dashboards across projects
description:
Users with multiple projects wanted to copy an entire dashboard (including all its insights and filters) from one project to another. Previously they had to recreate each insight manually.

Implemented a "Duplicate to project" option in the dashboard menu that deep-copies all tiles, saved insights, and dashboard-level filters to the target project. Person and cohort references are mapped to equivalents in the target project where possible, with a summary of what could not be mapped shown after duplication.

Shipped in v1.52.0. Confirmed working for dashboards with up to 30 tiles across Cloud and self-hosted deployments.

title: Toolbar not loading on sites with strict CSP headers
description:
The PostHog toolbar fails to initialize on our production site because our Content Security Policy blocks the injected script. We see a CSP violation error in the console:

Refused to load the script because it violates the following Content Security Policy directive: script-src 'self'

Canceled as duplicate of POS-412 which already tracks CSP compatibility improvements for the toolbar. The fix there involves loading the toolbar through a first-party proxy endpoint.

title: Support MongoDB as an event ingestion backend
description:
We would like to use MongoDB instead of ClickHouse for event storage. Our team has deep MongoDB expertise and we already run a large MongoDB cluster. It would simplify our infrastructure if PostHog could write events directly to our existing MongoDB deployment.

Canceled after evaluation: ClickHouse is fundamental to PostHog query performance and the entire query engine (HogQL) is built around its SQL dialect and columnar storage model. Supporting MongoDB would require rewriting the analytics query layer, aggregation pipeline, and materialized views. The performance characteristics would not meet the interactive query SLAs that PostHog users expect. Recommended using the batch export feature to replicate data to MongoDB for custom use cases instead.
